summary.intent: Fix review comments on PR #678 in fogodev/ars-ui and get it approved by Codex with clean CI.
summary.outcome: Three review threads were resolved with commit a9258202, but Clippy CI failed on collapsible-if lint in the new restore-clearing branches; a follow-up clippy/fmt fix was committed and pushed.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask ci clippy` as the workspace-wide Clippy gate — running only focused tests locally does not catch this.
Nightly fmt (`cargo +nightly fmt --all`) is required and enforced in CI; a single line-wrap difference will fail the check.
Spec files in spec/components/layout/ must be kept in sync with implementation changes and are validated via `cargo xtask spec validate`.
Codex reviews are triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment, and its verdict is tracked via GitHub reactions (+1 for approval, eyes for in-progress).
Review threads are resolved via the GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation paired with a reply comment citing the fixing commit.

code.finding: New restore-clearing branches triggered clippy::collapsible-if; nested if conditions must be collapsed into a single compound condition to pass the workspace Clippy gate.
Spec snippet for the restore-clearing logic needed to mirror the code restructuring to pass `cargo xtask spec validate`.

workflow: Passive CI polling with `gh pr checks 678 --watch` and a background reaction-polling loop can be killed with pkill before switching to active debugging.
The systematic-debugging workflow (get exact failure → map to local change → reproduce/fix locally → push minimal repair) is encoded in ~/.claude/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md.
Committing with `--no-gpg-sign` is used in this repo when adding the Codex co-author trailer.

friction: Local focused tests (`cargo test -p ars-components layout::splitter`) do not run the workspace Clippy gate, so the collapsible-if lint was missed before pushing.
Nightly fmt introduced a line-wrap difference that required a separate format pass after the clippy fix, adding a small extra cycle.
Two separate background polling processes (CI watch + reaction poll) had to be manually killed via pkill before switching to active debugging.
open_items: Codex had not yet emitted an approval reaction at the time the transcript ends — verdict for the new head (post clippy fix commit) is still pending.
It is unknown whether the clippy/fmt fix commit was successfully pushed and whether CI passed on the final head.
